task_completion.description: Production data is loaded into Power BI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Fields/Data pane on the right side of Power BI shows the imported table (e.g., production_log) with its columns.

task_completion.description: The four required measures are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Fields/Data pane shows the measures: Availability_Pct, Quality_Pct, Performance_Pct, and OEE_Score, indicated by calculator icons next to their names.

task_completion.description: A Gauge Chart is created for OEE_Score with a target of 0.85.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Gauge visual is present on the report canvas displaying the OEE_Score. A target line or value of 0.85 (or 85%) is visible on the gauge.

task_completion.description: A Matrix visual is created displaying Machine_ID and the four measures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Matrix visual is present on the report canvas. The rows display Machine_IDs (M-101, M-102, etc.), and the columns/values show the calculated results for Availability_Pct, Quality_Pct, Performance_Pct, and OEE_Score.

task_completion.description: The Power BI report is saved as OEE_Dashboard.pbix.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Power BI application title bar displays 'OEE_Dashboard' or there is a file named 'OEE_Dashboard.pbix' visible on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: The summary data is exported to machine_oee_summary.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'machine_oee_summary.csv' is visible on the Desktop or in the File Explorer, or the Power BI 'Export data' dialog is shown saving this file.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Power BI Desktop GUI to create the report and visuals.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Power BI interface being actively used (e.g., dragging fields, writing DAX formulas in the formula bar).

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script or terminal commands to bypass Power BI and directly generate the .pbix or .csv files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows running scripts to create the final outputs without using the Power BI application.

integrity.description: The CSV file was exported from the Power BI Matrix visual, not manually created.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'Export data' option being selected from the Matrix visual's menu in Power BI, or the export dialog.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 50 records across 5 machines (M-101, M-102, M-103, M-201, M-202). The OEE_Score is calculated as Availability_Pct * Quality_Pct * Performance_Pct. The Gauge chart must have a target of 0.85. The Matrix visual must show Machine_ID as rows and the 4 measures as values. The final files should be OEE_Dashboard.pbix and machine_oee_summary.csv on the Desktop. The verifier accepts solutions within 1% tolerance of calculated OEE scores (0.01 absolute difference).
